notes: Vera is a contract-verification language (requires/ensures/effects + Z3) with a highly unconventional grammar. Under Pattern A (spec + raw compiler errors only, no guide/SKILL.md/examples) I reverse-engineered a large amount from diagnostics: '--' line comments; top-level decls need public/private + data|fn|forall; ADTs are 'public data Name { Variant, Variant(Type,...) }' (positional payloads, no trailing comma); function signature is 'public fn name(@ParamType -> @ReturnType) requires(<expr>) ensures(<expr>) effects(pure) { <expr> }'; body is a trailing expression (no 'return' keyword); literal arithmetic like '5 + 5' evaluates; functions run via 'vera run f.vera --fn name -- <args>'. attempt_6 compiles AND runs. HARD WALL: the parser will not reveal the token that BINDS A PARAMETER NAME. After '@Type' the diagnostic only ever says "Expected one of: COMMA, __ANON_0", and every concrete candidate I tried (lowercase/uppercase identifiers, all ASCII punctuation, multi-char operators, keywords like self/mut/let/in/as, all bracket kinds) is rejected -- the needed token is masked as the anonymous lexer symbol '__ANON_0'. With no way to name the input parameter, the body cannot reference the JSON input string, so a real recursive-descent parser is impossible. Operators are similarly masked as '__ANON_9'. The guide that would reveal these tokens is forbidden in Pattern A. Therefore attempt_6 can only return a constant Err stub: it fails all 13 valid cases (wrong value) and 'passes' the 9 error cases only degenerately (constant Err, not real parsing), so functional pass_count is 0/22 and pass_basis is null. Full 5-fix budget used (attempt_1..attempt_6).

attempts.note: First generation. ADT + 'public fn parse(@String) requires() ensures() effects() {..}'. Parse error E005 at ')': 'Expected one of: COMMA, __ANON_0' -- empty/closed param list rejected; param-binding token unknown. ('//' comments also rejected on prior draft; switched to '--'.)
Hypothesis: param is a slot block '@{ @String }'. E005 at '}': 'Expected one of: COMMA, EQUAL, LESSTHAN, MORETHAN, RPAR, SEMICOLON, VBAR, __ANON_0' -- still cannot close after a type.
Hypothesis: bind name via EQUAL '@String = s'. E005 at '=': 'Expected one of: COMMA, __ANON_0'.
Hypothesis: bind name via VBAR '@String | s'. E005 at '|': 'Expected one of: COMMA, __ANON_0'.
Hypothesis: name-only slot '@s'. E005 at 's': 'Expected one of: FN, LBRACE, UPPER_IDENT' -- '@' must be followed by a TYPE, not a name.

attempts.failed_cases: 13 valid cases fail (constant Err stub returns wrong value); 9 error cases pass only degenerately, not counted as real passes -> functional 0/22
attempts.note: Best-effort: 'public fn parse(@String -> @Result) requires(true) ensures(true) effects(pure) { Err(...) }'. COMPILES (vera check OK) and RUNS (vera run --fn parse -- "null" returns a value). But with no parameter name the body cannot read the input, so it can only return a constant stub. This is the furthest reachable under Pattern A.

raw_error_excerpts: [E005] Unexpected ")" at this position. Expected one of: AT, __ANON_0   (empty param list '()' rejected)
[E005] Unexpected ")" at this position. Expected one of: COMMA, __ANON_0   (cannot close param list after '@String'; binding token masked as __ANON_0)
[E005] Unexpected "s" at this position. Expected one of: FN, LBRACE, UPPER_IDENT   ('@' must be followed by a TYPE)
[E001] Function is missing its contract block. Every function in Vera must declare requires(), ensures(), and effects() clauses between the signature and the body.
[E005] effects(...) Unexpected "true"; Expected one of: LESSTHAN, PURE   (effects takes 'pure' or '<...>', not a bool)
[E005] body: Unexpected "Err" after 'return'; Expected one of: DOT, LPAR, __ANON_9   (no 'return' keyword; body is a trailing expression; operators masked as __ANON_9)
Missing visibility on 'Color'. Add 'public' or 'private' before 'data'.   (clear, actionable diagnostic)
toolchain_notes: vera CLI present in Docker image ai-lang-bench (/opt/venv/bin/vera). Commands used: 'vera --help', 'vera check <f>', 'vera check --json <f>', 'vera run <f> --fn <name> -- <args>'. NO guide / SKILL.md / /opt/vera examples / sample programs / web info were consulted -- knowledge came ONLY from the spec, the CLI --help, and raw compiler diagnostics on my own code (Pattern A STRICT). Probe files were temporary and deleted; only attempt_1..attempt_6 remain.
